subject: Physics
title: Light as a Particle
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Light as a Particle.

hook.text: A century after Young proved light is a wave, Einstein proved it's also a particle.

buildup.text: When light hits metal, electrons fly off. Brighter light frees more electrons but doesn't speed them up.

discovery.text: Einstein said light comes in packets—photons. Each photon's energy depends on frequency, not brightness.

twist.text: This meant light is somehow both a wave and a particle. Nobody expected nature to be so contradictory.

climax.text: The photon concept earned Einstein the Nobel Prize and launched quantum physics.

punchline.text: Wave and particle. Light plays by its own rules.

quiz.question: What aspect of light did Einstein prove with the photoelectric effect?
options: Light comes in discrete packets called photons
Light always behaves as a wave
